data.description:
## Summary
`rebar show <id>` intermittently returns EMPTY stdout with an apparently-successful (zero) exit instead of the
ticket JSON. Downstream consumers that pipe/redirect `show` into a parser receive empty input and crash. The
store itself is intact â€” the same ticket `show`s correctly moments later, and `fsck` is clean â€” so this is a
read-output bug, not data loss.

## Circumstances under which we observed it
Seen repeatedly during a session doing scripted readâ†’transformâ†’write loops on tickets (fetch a description with
`rebar show`, edit it, push with `rebar edit --description`):
- It manifests during a BURST of rapid back-to-back rebar invocations â€” several `rebar edit` (writes) immediately
  followed by `rebar show` (reads) â€” with `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` (the default), so each write spawns a
  background push to `origin/tickets`.
- The empty return correlates with those in-flight background pushes/git activity on the `tickets` worktree
  (`.tickets-tracker/`): a tight retry loop of `rebar show <id> > file` can fail on EVERY attempt (8/8) during the
  contention window, while a single `rebar show` after a multi-second quiet period succeeds.
- DECISIVE SIGNAL: setting `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` for the read/write burst made `rebar show` succeed reliably every
  time (single piped call) â€” strongly implicating background-push / git-lock contention as the cause.
- During the same window, `rebar clarity-check <id>` and `rebar check-ac <id>` on the SAME id SUCCEED (a different
  read path), and `rebar fsck` reports "no issues found" â€” so the data is present; only `show`'s stdout comes back
  empty.
- Environment: macOS (darwin) / zsh; rebar over the `tickets` orphan branch (worktree `.tickets-tracker/`); an
  `origin` remote present; default `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`.

## Reproduction Steps
1. With `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` (default) and an `origin` remote, run a burst: `rebar edit` on several tickets,
   then immediately `rebar show <id> > out.json` (or `rebar show <id> | python3 -c "import sys,json;json.load(sys.stdin)"`).
2. Repeat in a tight loop.
3. Observe `out.json` intermittently empty / the parser failing with `Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)`,
   despite a success-looking exit.
4. Set `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and repeat â†’ `rebar show` succeeds every time.

## Expected vs Actual
- EXPECTED: `rebar show` is read-consistent under concurrent background push â€” it returns the full ticket JSON,
  or on genuine failure exits NON-ZERO with a diagnostic on stderr. It must NEVER emit empty stdout with exit 0.
- ACTUAL: intermittently emits empty stdout with a success exit during background-push contention, silently
  breaking readâ†’transformâ†’write pipelines.

## Impact
- Silently breaks scripted pipelines that consume `rebar show`. It is also a latent DATA-LOSS vector in
  combination with `edit --description`: an empty `show` feeding an unguarded `edit --description "$(...)"` would
  erase content â€” the edit-side empty-value guard (bug `e78f-9f79`) mitigates the clobber, but the empty `show`
  is the upstream root cause and should be fixed directly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar show` never returns empty stdout on a zero exit; under read/push contention it returns the complete
      record or exits non-zero with a clear error to stderr.
- [ ] `show` reads a consistent snapshot (or retries internally) so a concurrent background push to
      `origin/tickets` cannot truncate its output; reproduced + fixed under `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`.
- [ ] A regression test exercises the burst-of-writes-then-`show` pattern and asserts `show` output is always
      complete-or-erroring (never empty+exit0).
- [ ] Audit the other read paths (clarity-check/check-ac/fsck observed unaffected) for the same race; fix
      uniformly or document why only `show` is affected.
data.title: rebar show intermittently returns empty stdout (exit 0) under concurrent background auto-push
